name: Circular Route 3 da Ericeira
region_intro: Touring cycling around Ericeira offers diverse landscapes, from its Atlantic coastline defined by high cliffs and picturesque bays to inland rolling hills and verdant woods. The region features the Lizandro river valley and is bordered by natural parks such as Sintra-Cascais and Mafra. Coastal routes often provide views of the designated World Surfing Reserve, while inland paths explore varied terrain. This area provides a range of routes suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Ericeira The most popular touring cycling route is Circular Route 3 da Ericeira, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour to complete. This route offers a local loop with varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Azenhas do Mar – Azenhas do Mar loop from Ericeira, a difficult 28.8 miles (46.3 km) path. This route features coastal views towards the village of Azenhas do Mar. Local touring cyclists also love the The Surfer – Santa Rita Beach loop from Ericeira, a 40.2 miles (64.6 km) trail leading through coastal areas and past surf spots, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Ericeira is defined by coastal roads, rolling hills, and the Lizandro river valley. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Ericeira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ericeira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Ericeira?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Ericeira, offering a variety of experiences from coastal rides to inland explorations. These routes are rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Ericeira?
A: Ericeira's touring cycling routes feature diverse terrain. You'll find stunning coastal roads with ocean views, rolling hills inland, and paths through verdant woods and the Lizandro river valley. The region offers a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging routes, with 3 easy, 8 moderate, and 17 difficult options.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling routes in Ericeira?
A: Yes, Ericeira offers several routes suitable for families, particularly those looking for easier rides. There are 3 easy touring cycling routes available, which often include less elevation and shorter distances, perfect for a leisurely family outing.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Ericeira?
A: Absolutely. Many touring cycling routes in Ericeira are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2292722065/circular-route-3-da-ericeira" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Route 3 da Ericeira&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 14.8 km trail that takes about an hour to complete.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Ericeira's routes offer many scenic points. You can cycle past the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2668348" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz do Lizandro Beach Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, explore historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2560455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forte do Milreu&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mafra National Palace&lt;/a&gt;. Coastal routes often provide views of the World Surfing Reserve and beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1839341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia do Sul&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Ericeira has 17 difficult touring cycling routes for experienced riders. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17311884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azenhas do Mar – Praia de São Julião loop from Ericeira&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 70.7 km route with significant elevation changes, offering a rewarding experience for advanced cyclists.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about cycling in Ericeira?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ericeira are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning coastal views, the variety of terrain, and the opportunity to explore charming villages and natural parks.

Q: Can I combine cycling with cultural experiences in Ericeira?
A: Absolutely. Many routes allow you to combine cycling with cultural stops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10346006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Surfer – Mafra National Palace loop from Ericeira&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mafra National Palace&lt;/a&gt;. The charming fishing village of Ericeira itself, with its cobblestone streets, is also a great place to explore by bike.

Q: Are there routes that offer views of the famous surf spots?
A: Yes, Ericeira is a designated World Surfing Reserve, and several coastal cycling paths offer views of popular surf spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32272157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Surfer – Santa Rita Beach loop from Ericeira&lt;/a&gt; is a 64.6 km trail that leads through coastal areas and past these dynamic surf locations.

Q: What are some natural parks accessible by bike from Ericeira?
A: Ericeira is conveniently located near significant natural parks. The Sintra-Cascais Natural Park offers diverse flora and fauna, while the National Park of Mafra, which includes areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6302068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tapada Nacional de Mafra&lt;/a&gt;, provides additional opportunities for nature exploration and can be reached via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10346006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Surfer – Mafra National Palace loop from Ericeira&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that take me to other coastal towns or villages?
A: Yes, you can find routes that extend to other picturesque coastal destinations. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14664167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Azenhas do Mar – Azenhas do Mar loop from Ericeira&lt;/a&gt; is a 46.3 km path that features coastal views towards the charming village of Azenhas do Mar.

Q: Where can I find more information about local attractions in Ericeira?
A: For more details on local attractions and things to do in Ericeira, you can visit the official tourism guide at &lt;a href="https://www.ericeiraportugaltourism.com/guide/top-10-things-to-see-and-do-in-ericeira.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;ericeiraportugaltourism.com&lt;/a&gt;. This can help you plan stops along your cycling routes.
tips: A small chapel located right next to one of Ericeira's larger parking lots. It's beautiful from the outside, with its golden roof, but its true beauty unfolds inside. Be sure to go inside.
Scenic overlook from the old town high above the cliffs down to the sandy beach
Ericeira's main beach, although this is more of a bathing spot, the bigger waves are in other places. But you can also see a few surfers here and there practicing riding the waves.
Panorama viewpoints from the high cliffs above the mouth of the Lizandro river, with the sandy beach on the Atlantic ocean.
